pr:section.pr:title: XML Templates
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Canonical XML boilerplate for creating clayers elements. Referenced by layer mode guidance.

cnt:content.cnt:body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <spec:clayers xmlns:spec="urn:clayers:spec" xmlns:pr="urn:clayers:prose" xmlns:trm="urn:clayers:terminology" xmlns:org="urn:clayers:organization" xmlns:rel="urn:clayers:relation" xmlns:art="urn:clayers:artifact" xmlns:llm="urn:clayers:llm" spec:index="index.xml">
<!-- Content goes here -->
</spec:clayers>

<trm:term id="term-CONCEPT-NAME"> <trm:name>Concept Name</trm:name> <trm:definition>A precise, self-contained definition that distinguishes this concept from similar ones. Should be understandable without reading any other part of the spec.</trm:definition> </trm:term>

<pr:section id="section-MODULE-NAME"> <pr:title>Module Name</pr:title> <pr:shortdesc>One-sentence summary of what this module does and why it exists.</pr:shortdesc>
<pr:p>Explain the module's purpose, design rationale, and how it fits into the larger system. Focus on intent ("why") rather than implementation details ("what the code does").</pr:p> </pr:section>

<!-- Use concept for explanatory content --> <org:concept ref="section-ID"> <org:purpose>Understanding how X works and why it was designed this way.</org:purpose> </org:concept>
<!-- Use task for system behavior --> <org:task ref="section-ID"> <org:purpose>The system performs Y when Z happens.</org:purpose> </org:task>
<!-- Use reference for lookup information --> <org:reference ref="section-ID"> <org:purpose>Quick lookup for API endpoints / configuration keys / error codes.</org:purpose> </org:reference>

<rel:relation type="depends-on" from="SOURCE-ID" to="TARGET-ID"> <rel:note>Brief explanation of why this dependency exists.</rel:note> </rel:relation>
<!-- Relation types: depends-on : SOURCE requires TARGET to function refines : SOURCE is a more specific version of TARGET references : SOURCE mentions or uses TARGET precedes : SOURCE must happen before TARGET implements : SOURCE realizes the concept in TARGET constrains : SOURCE limits what TARGET can do reverses : SOURCE undoes or opposes TARGET -->

<art:mapping id="map-CONCEPT-NAME"> <art:spec-ref node="NODE-ID" revision="draft-1" node-hash="sha256:placeholder"/> <art:artifact repo="REPO-ID" repo-revision="COMMIT-HASH" path="path/to/file.rs"> <art:range hash="sha256:placeholder" start-line="10" end-line="40"/> </art:artifact> <art:coverage>full</art:coverage> <art:note>Brief description of what this code implements from the spec node.</art:note> </art:mapping>
<art:exempt node="NODE-ID" revision="draft-1"> <art:reason>This node is an abstract concept / organizational element with no direct code implementation.</art:reason> </art:exempt>

<!-- Describe a spec node for LLM consumption --> <llm:node ref="NODE-ID"> Explain what this node means, how it relates to other nodes, and guidance for interpreting or modifying it. Start with the most important information. </llm:node>
<!-- Describe a schema (layer) for LLM consumption --> <llm:schema namespace="urn:clayers:LAYER-NAME"> Explain the layer's purpose, its main elements, and how they relate to other layers. Include guidance on when to use this layer and common patterns. </llm:schema>

<dec:decision ref="section-DECISION-NAME" id="dec-DECISION-NAME"> <dec:status>proposed</dec:status> <dec:date>YYYY-MM-DD</dec:date> <dec:alternative id="alt-OPTION-NAME"> <dec:title>Option name</dec:title> <dec:rationale>Why this option was rejected.</dec:rationale> </dec:alternative> </dec:decision>

<pln:plan id="plan-FEATURE-NAME"> <pln:title>Feature implementation plan</pln:title> <pln:overview>Brief description of what this plan achieves.</pln:overview> <pln:status>proposed</pln:status> <pln:item id="item-STEP-NAME"> <pln:title>Step description</pln:title> <pln:description>What to do in this step.</pln:description> <pln:acceptance> <pln:criterion>The expected outcome is verifiable.</pln:criterion> </pln:acceptance> <pln:item-status>pending</pln:item-status> </pln:item> </pln:plan>

spec:clayers.llm:node: XML boilerplate templates for creating clayers elements. Each cnt:content element shows the canonical structure for a specific element type. Mode guidance references these via pr:xref so agents can copy and adapt the templates during onboarding.
